message:
feat(trauma_guard): add mutation-epoch tracking to reset loop detection on source edits

Introduce a mutation-epoch mechanism that distinguishes between an agent
blindly retrying the same failing command versus one that is actually
editing source code between attempts. When the agent modifies functional
files (source, config, build files) between retries, the repeat-loop
counter resets to 1 — preventing false intervention on legitimate
iterative development.

Core additions:
- mutation_epoch field on TraumaState, TraumaEvent, and CommandFeatures
  to track the monotonically increasing edit generation
- record_mutation(timestamp, path) increments the epoch for functional
  file mutations but filters out scratchpad/docs mutations via
  is_functional_mutation_path() — files under .beads/, *.md, *.txt,
  *.log, and other non-functional paths are excluded
- is_trivial_variation() now checks epoch equality: if the mutation
  epoch differs between the current command and a historical event,
  the commands are NOT considered trivial variations even if they are
  textually identical — the agent demonstrably changed something
- When epoch mismatch is detected, the repeat chain breaks and the
  loop counter resets, preventing false trauma guard intervention

New tests:
- source_mutation_resets_repeat_chain: verifies that editing .rs files
  between retries resets the loop counter
- scratchpad_mutation_does_not_reset: .beads/ writes don't reset
- docs_mutation_does_not_reset: .md file edits don't reset
- e2e_mutation_interleaved_loop_bounded: full scenario with interleaved
  mutations and command retries
- e2e_functional_edit_recovery_sequence: realistic recovery scenario
- proptest: interleaved command/error streams with mutations stay
  bounded, functional mutation filter on source-like paths

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
